top_signals.title: Two Chinese VLCCs Exit Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Two Chinese supertankers carrying 4 million barrels of Middle East crude oil exited the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday after waiting in the Gulf for more than two months, shipping data on LSEG and Kpler showed.The ships are among a handful of supertankers carrying Iraqi crude exiting the Gulf this mo
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: BofA Sees Three Catalysts That Could Turn It Bullish on Yen
top_signals.summary: BofA Securities is turning less bearish on the yen and sees three catalysts that could eventually turn it outright bullish, even as the currency drifts back toward 160 per dollar.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Data shows Bitcoin dip buyers waiting for lower prices: Is $70K BTC’s next stop?
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin futures and orderbook data show dip buyers waiting for a BTC price drop below $70,000.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Indonesian Markets Rebound Ahead of Prabowo’s Parliament Speech
top_signals.summary: Indonesia’s currency and stocks rebounded Wednesday in volatile trade, erasing earlier losses on optimism ahead of a key budget speech by President Prabowo Subianto.

top_signals.title: WTO chief urges Japan to lead the charge in reforming its rules
top_signals.summary: As an "important middle power," Japan should take the lead in reforms for the World Trade Organization, said Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala, its director-general.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
